symptoms: Dark brown, concentric spots (target board effect) on older leaves.
Spots may merge, causing leaves to yellow, dry up, and drop off.
Sunken, brown, dry spots may also appear on the tubers.
causes: Favored by alternating wet and dry weather conditions.
Poor soil nutrition, plant stress, or aging foliage.
treatmentOrganic: Apply organic copper-based fungicides or bio-fungicides.
Spray neem oil or compost tea to suppress fungal growth.
Prune and destroy infected lower leaves immediately to prevent upward spread.
treatmentChemical: Apply contact fungicides containing Chlorothalonil, Mancozeb, or Propineb.
Use systemic fungicides like Azoxystrobin or Difenoconazole for severe infections.
prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds.
Practice crop rotation (avoid nightshades like tomato/eggplant in the same soil).
Provide adequate spacing between plants to maximize air circulation.
Water at the base of the plants using drip irrigation to avoid leaf wetness.

symptoms: Large, irregular water-soaked spots on leaves that rapidly turn brown/black.
A white, downy mold-like growth appears on the undersides of leaves in humid weather.
Rapid collapse and rotting of foliage; tubers develop a dark, dry rot.
causes: Cool (10-24°C), wet, and highly humid (>90%) weather.
Wind-borne spores traveling long distances from neighboring infected fields.
treatmentOrganic: Spray copper fungicides preventatively before the disease spreads.
Remove and destroy infected plants completely (do not compost).
Apply bio-agents like Bacillus subtilis to enhance resistance.
treatmentChemical: Apply systemic fungicides containing Metalaxyl, Cymoxanil, or Dimethomorph.
Spray immediately on receiving high-humidity weather warnings.
prevention: Plant late blight-resistant potato cultivars.
Destroy volunteer potato plants and cull piles before the season starts.
Hill potatoes adequately to protect tubers from washing spores.
Harvest only when vines have been completely dead for 2 weeks.

symptoms: Leaves are vibrant green, flat, and free of spots or discoloration.
Tubers are clean, firm, and developing normally.
causes: Effective disease management and healthy seed stock.
prevention: Maintain good watering schedule.
Conduct regular checks for early signs of pests.

symptoms: Dark spots with concentric rings (target pattern) on older leaves.
Leaves turn yellow and fall off, leading to sunscald on the fruit.
Sunken, leathery dark spots on the stem end of tomato fruit.
causes: High humidity, heavy dews, and warm temperatures.
Spores overwintering in soil, crop residue, or weeds.
treatmentOrganic: Prune lower leaves to prevent splash-up from the soil.
Apply organic copper-based sprays or biofungicides.
Mulch around the base of the tomato plants immediately after planting.
treatmentChemical: Apply preventative contact fungicides like Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb.
Spray systemic fungicides if the disease progresses rapidly.
prevention: Ensure a 3-year crop rotation schedule.
Provide strong support stakes or cages to keep leaves off the wet ground.
Water only in the morning at the root zone (drip irrigation preferred).
Prune the bottom 12 inches of leaves as the plant grows.

symptoms: Large water-soaked spots on leaves that quickly expand and turn brown.
White fuzzy growth on the undersides of leaves during high humidity.
Large, firm, greasy-looking brown patches on tomato fruits.
causes: Consistently cool, wet, and humid conditions.
Overhead watering or closely crowded tomato plants.
treatmentOrganic: Remove and discard infected plant tissue immediately.
Apply copper fungicides as a preventative spray at first warning.
Use bio-control agents containing Bacillus subtilis.
treatmentChemical: Apply systemic and contact fungicides (e.g., Mefenoxam, Chlorothalonil).
Apply treatments every 5-7 days under wet weather patterns.
prevention: Grow resistant tomato varieties.
Avoid overhead watering and water late in the day.
Allow wide spacing between plants to speed up drying of foliage.
Avoid planting tomatoes near potatoes.

symptoms: Lush green leaves, robust yellow blossoms, and clean, firm fruit.
No leaf spots, curling, or dark patches on fruits.
causes: Optimized growing conditions and excellent plant hygiene.
treatmentOrganic: Apply liquid organic fertilizer or seaweed extract for growth support.
prevention: Maintain crop cage hygiene and regular weed clearance.
Inspect leaves weekly.
